programme.title: Seconds from Disaster 6
programme.desc: A reminder of the greatest disasters of our time.The program includes, among others:transport accidents, building collapses and terrorist attacks.

programme.title: Seconds from Disaster 6
programme.desc: A reminder of the greatest disasters of our time.The program includes, among others:transport accidents, building collapses and terrorist attacks.

programme.title: Air Crash Investigation
programme.desc: The series' creators solve the mysteries of the biggest plane crashes.Trying to recreate the events that took place before the tragedies, they reconstruct the situations that preceded them on board the machines.

programme.title: Air Crash Investigation
programme.desc: Behind the scenes of air accidents in recent years.Each episode will present a reconstruction of the events that led to the tragedy.The stories are supplemented by reports from experts and eyewitnesses.

programme.title: Drain the Oceans
programme.desc: The sea depths hide many secrets.The latest technologies will allow you to show wrecks, sunken treasures, cities and even surprising geological formations.One of the puzzles is the sinking of the unfinished aircraft carrier Graf Zeppelin.Scientists will also embark on a journey across the Pacific Ocean to explore the Pacific Ring of Fire - a region of frequent earthquakes and volcanic eruptions.In addition, specialists will analyze the history of wars, piracy and slavery in the Gulf of Mexico region and the collapse of the largest ancient civilizations in the Mediterranean basin.

credits.actor: Self - Narrator: National Geographic Channel - Russell Boulter
Self - Norwegian University of Science and Technology - Fredrik Soreide
Self - Author and Historian - Craig L. Symonds

programme.title: Unearth
programme.desc: We set off on a journey to the world of science, space and technology.During this expedition, we learn more about the discoveries of our past and visions for the future.

programme.title: Inside Airport Lost \u0026 Found
programme.sub-title: An expensive watch and priceless pearls
programme.desc: Behind the scenes of the work of the lost and found office.Search teams make every effort to return the most surprising lost items to travelers.These include artificial eyes, luxury watches and ritual robes.

programme.title: Toronto Airport Uncovered
programme.desc: Behind the scenes of the operation of the largest airport in Canada.Viewers will see how the staff ensures traffic flows during record snowfall and negative temperatures.

programme.title: Iceland: Cold War Frontier
programme.desc: The fact that foreign troops were stationed in Iceland for 55 years as part of their obligations to the North Atlantic Alliance had a significant impact on the culture, politics and economy of the island.

programme.title: To Catch A Smuggler 5
programme.desc: It is estimated that 124 million people fly to the United States every year.Among them are smugglers and criminals.Employees of the US Department of Homeland Security are tracking them and protecting the border.

programme.title: Genghis Khan: the Secret History of the Mongols
programme.desc: A profile of a man who went down in history as a brilliant military strategist.The six-episode series will present little-known facts from the life of the fearless leader.The authors will show how, after a difficult childhood and captivity, he managed to gain power, unite the quarreling tribes, build an invincible army and subjugate all those who opposed him, including his former allies.Impressive conquests will be discussed, including:expansion into the West, campaigns against the neighboring Tangut people and the Chinese Jin Empire.The troops he commanded were known for the mobility of their cavalry, carrying out rapid attacks and retreats, and applying psychological pressure on their opponents.They used innovative techniques during sieges of even well-fortified cities.Genghis Khan became one of the most important figures in history, expanding his empire to unprecedented sizes.It stretched from the Pacific to the Caspian and Black Seas.The creators of the series are based on a new translation of the anonymous chronicle "The Secret History of the Mongols", which is the oldest known written monument of the steppe peoples of Central Asia.

credits.actor: Self - French Institute for Central Asian Studies - Marie Favereau
Self - National University of Mongolia - Bayarsaikhan Dashdondog

programme.title: Chernobyl: Inside the Meltdown
programme.desc: On April 26, 1986, an explosion destroyed the fourth block of the Chernobyl power plant.The accident was caused by design defects in the reactor and errors during the safety test.It was the worst nuclear disaster in history.The explosion released 400 times more radiation than the bomb dropped on Hiroshima.40 years after the tragedy, its consequences will be shown - from KGB activities to military operations in Ukraine.There will be plenty of witness accounts that will shed new light on the dramatic events.The speakers will include:liquidator working on the roof of reactor 3 and journalist Wyatt Andrews, who was covering the information blockade in Moscow On April 8, the remains of the fourth reactor were still unextinguished.The authorities finally decide to evacuate.50,000 people take supplies for three days.They don't know that they will never return to their homes.Meanwhile, firefighters with burns are being watched by the KGB.Swedish technicians at the Forsmark nuclear power plant are the first to detect increased levels of radiation.They alert the world about the catastrophe that the USSR authorities are trying to hide.Events two weeks after the explosion will be discussed.The authors will explain how attempts were made to hide the truth.

programme.title: Nazi Megastructures: Russia's War
programme.desc: One of Hitler's aspirations was to liquidate the Russian nation in order to gain living space for the Aryans.The German army reached the outskirts of Moscow, but the Red Army managed to stop the attack.

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering: Season 13
programme.desc: There are many engineering projects in the world that have been abandoned for various reasons.The creators of the program present the stories of these structures and the people who created them.Behind each of them there is a fascinating and unique story.Viewers will discover the most spectacular examples of abandoned buildings from around the globe, including an underground Nazi base in France, designed to attack London, and a secret Japanese facility.We will find out why they were created and what led to their fall.

programme.title: Chernobyl: Inside the Meltdown
programme.desc: On April 26, 1986, an explosion destroyed the fourth block of the Chernobyl power plant.The accident was caused by design defects in the reactor and errors during the safety test.It was the worst nuclear disaster in history.The explosion released 400 times more radiation than the bomb dropped on Hiroshima.40 years after the tragedy, its consequences will be shown - from KGB activities to military operations in Ukraine.There will be plenty of witness accounts that will shed new light on the dramatic events.The speakers will include:a liquidator working on the roof of reactor 3, and journalist Wyatt Andrews, who was covering the information blackout in Moscow.

programme.title: Air Crash Investigation
programme.desc: The 11th series of a series whose creators try to solve the mysteries of the biggest plane crashes.Thanks to the opinions of experts and eyewitness accounts, the events before the tragedy will be recreated.

programme.title: Air Crash Investigation 21
programme.desc: Reconstructions of real plane crashes.By recreating the course of events, the program's creators try to understand what led to the unfortunate events.They recreate the circumstances in which the tragedy occurred: the weather, the atmosphere on board the plane, the decisions and actions of the pilots.They also show the work of people investigating the causes of disasters and conduct a computer flight simulation.Viewers can hear various comments from specialists and testimonies of witnesses to these events.The creators of the program will prove that air accidents are often caused by human slowness and lack of imagination.Each disaster is thoroughly analyzed and the lessons learned help minimize the number of tragedies in the future.

programme.title: Seconds from Disaster 6
programme.desc: A reminder of the greatest disasters of our time.The program includes, among others:transport accidents, building collapses and terrorist attacks.

programme.title: Destruction Decoded
programme.desc: The creators present recordings that show man's clash with the power of the elements.The combination of archival materials and special effects allows you to move right into the middle of dramatic events.

programme.title: Air Crash Investigation
programme.desc: The series' creators solve the mysteries of the biggest plane crashes.Trying to recreate the events that took place before the tragedies, they reconstruct the situations that preceded them on board the machines.

programme.title: Air Crash Investigation
programme.desc: Behind the scenes of air accidents in recent years.Each episode will present a reconstruction of the events that led to the tragedy.The stories are supplemented by reports from experts and eyewitnesses.

programme.title: Drain the Oceans
programme.desc: The sea depths hide many secrets.The latest technologies will allow you to show wrecks, sunken treasures, cities and even surprising geological formations.One of the puzzles is the sinking of the unfinished aircraft carrier Graf Zeppelin.Scientists will also embark on a journey across the Pacific Ocean to explore the Pacific Ring of Fire - a region of frequent earthquakes and volcanic eruptions.In addition, specialists will analyze the history of wars, piracy and slavery in the Gulf of Mexico region and the collapse of the largest ancient civilizations in the Mediterranean basin.

credits.actor: Self - Narrator: National Geographic Channel - Russell Boulter
Self - Norwegian University of Science and Technology - Fredrik Soreide
Self - Author and Historian - Craig L. Symonds

programme.title: Unearth
programme.desc: We set off on a journey to the world of science, space and technology.During this expedition, we learn more about the discoveries of our past and visions for the future.

programme.title: Inside Airport Lost \u0026 Found
programme.sub-title: Old photos and a new student
programme.desc: Behind the scenes of the work of the lost and found office.Search teams make every effort to return the most surprising lost items to travelers.These include artificial eyes, luxury watches and ritual robes.

programme.title: Toronto Airport Uncovered
programme.desc: Behind the scenes of the operation of the largest airport in Canada.Viewers will see how the staff ensures traffic flows during record snowfall and negative temperatures.

programme.title: Evolution of Evil
programme.desc: Portraits of famous despots.Psychopathic, greedy, sometimes simply crazy, they have taken control of the country and are terrorizing their subjects.The creators of the program are looking for the sources of their behavior.

programme.title: Evolution of Evil
programme.desc: Portraits of famous despots.Psychopathic, greedy, sometimes simply crazy, they have taken control of the country and are terrorizing their subjects.The creators of the program are looking for the sources of their behavior.

programme.title: To Catch A Smuggler 5
programme.desc: It is estimated that 124 million people fly to the United States every year.Among them are smugglers and criminals.Employees of the US Department of Homeland Security are tracking them and protecting the border.

programme.title: Genghis Khan: the Secret History of the Mongols
programme.desc: A profile of a man who went down in history as a brilliant military strategist.The six-episode series will present little-known facts from the life of the fearless leader.The authors will show how, after a difficult childhood and captivity, he managed to gain power, unite the quarreling tribes, build an invincible army and subjugate all those who opposed him, including his former allies.Impressive conquests will be discussed, including:expansion into the West, campaigns against the neighboring Tangut people and the Chinese Jin Empire.The troops he commanded were known for the mobility of their cavalry, carrying out rapid attacks and retreats, and applying psychological pressure on their opponents.They used innovative techniques during sieges of even well-fortified cities.Genghis Khan became one of the most important figures in history, expanding his empire to unprecedented sizes.It stretched from the Pacific to the Caspian and Black Seas.The creators of the series are based on a new translation of the anonymous chronicle "The Secret History of the Mongols", which is the oldest known written monument of the steppe peoples of Central Asia.

credits.actor: Self - French Institute for Central Asian Studies - Marie Favereau
Self - National University of Mongolia - Bayarsaikhan Dashdondog

programme.title: Chernobyl: Inside the Meltdown
programme.desc: On April 26, 1986, an explosion destroyed the fourth block of the Chernobyl power plant.The accident was caused by design defects in the reactor and errors during the safety test.It was the worst nuclear disaster in history.The explosion released 400 times more radiation than the bomb dropped on Hiroshima.40 years after the tragedy, its consequences will be shown - from KGB activities to military operations in Ukraine.There will be plenty of witness accounts that will shed new light on the dramatic events.The speakers will include:a liquidator working on the roof of reactor 3, and journalist Wyatt Andrews, who was covering the information blackout in Moscow.

programme.title: Nazi Megastructures: Death From Above Compilation Season
programme.desc: The success of the attack on Pearl Harbor or the Battle of Britain depended on technical superiority over the enemy.The authors will explain the secrets of the machines that caused the sky to catch fire.
